source_jobs.title: Robotics Lead (Boulder, CO)
source_jobs.description:
Do you want to be part of building the next generation of robots and see those robots out in the real world automating dangerous, monotonous tasks? If so, consider joining us at PickNik as a Robotics Lead. The Robotics Lead role is at the forefront of solving the hard problems to put robots into production.

PickNik Robotics is the company behind MoveIt Pro, a software platform that combines AI and advanced motion planning to make robots work in the real world. We're focused on pushing the boundaries of robotics — and showing what’s possible when robots are powered by great software. PickNik has taken our learnings from the past 10 years - working with Google, Amazon, Samsung, BMW, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and many startups - and distilled that knowledge into MoveIt Pro. Join us in helping companies deploy AI to robots fast and effectively, opening up new possibilities only limited by our customer’s imagination.

As a Robotics Lead at PickNik Robotics, you will help lead development of customer implementations of MoveIt Pro. You will develop MoveIt Pro http://picknik.ai/pro-based software applications and create integration solutions for our customers. This role is focused on customer delivery - creating software that will be used on robots around the world (and in outer space!).



Am I a good fit?

 - You love robotics and have a passion for collaborating with customers

 - You understand technical scoping and how to manage a technical team

 - You work efficiently, are ready to learn, and have great attention to detail

 - You have the ability to collaborate with teams around the world



Requirements

 - Driven to solve customer problems and see your work make an impact

 - Excited to work with new customers and multi-task delivery of cool robot software

 - Multi-disciplinary approach to technical problem solving and willingness to do what needs to be done - working with 3D files, writing code, debugging performance, working with hardware, etc.

 - Minimum 6 years of professional work experience

 - Knowledge of C++17 or newer

 - Skilled with at least one scripting language such as Python

 - Extensive knowledge of software development

 - Experience with full life-cycle library and application development, Git version control, software testing, and continuous integration.

 - Experience with communication middleware

 - Be able to travel approximately every other month to customer sites & conferences. Less than 30% of the time.

 - Live in the Boulder/Denver area and have the ability to be in-office at least 3 days per week.

 - Degree(s) in Computer Science, Robotics, Engineering, or a related field and/or equivalent industry experience.



Some preferred skills/experience

 - ROS, MoveIt 2, Nav2, Mujoco, or other robotic libraries are advantageous.

 - Demonstrated industry ability to deliver complex projects.

 - Familiarity with scalable deployment processes like Docker, Kubernetes, and Ansible.



Benefits:

 - Health, Dental, and Vision insurance

 - 401k Matching

 - Paid time off

 - Opportunity to share in the company’s success through equity



Minimum Salary

 - The minimum salary for this position is $140,000/year.

source_jobs.title: Robotics Software Engineer - Test & Quality (Boulder, CO)
source_jobs.description:
PickNik Robotics is redefining the robotics industry with MoveIt Pro, a first-of-its-kind platform that combines AI and motion planning to make robots work in the real world. Physical AI is reshaping the field: as foundation models and vision-language-action (VLA) policies move from the lab into production, MoveIt Pro combines these learned approaches with robust, safety-aware planning and control so robots can reliably perform real tasks. Built on over a decade of experience with companies like GM, Philips, Samsung, BMW, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and leading startups, MoveIt Pro brings proven capabilities into production. Join us in helping companies deploy AI to robots quickly and effectively, unlocking new possibilities. This is a challenging, high-impact role with the opportunity to do some of the most rewarding work of your career.

PickNik is an agentic-coding-forward company. We use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as part of our everyday development to ship and maintain high-quality software faster, and we expect engineers to adopt these tools and keep getting more effective with them.

As a Robotics Software Engineer - Test & Quality at PickNik Robotics, you will help raise the quality bar for MoveIt Pro across the full stack — from C++/ROS 2 Behavior Tree nodes, through the Python REST API, to the React frontend. You'll write automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests, build the test infrastructure and CI quality gates that make it low-friction for every engineer to test their work, and run the exploratory, smoke, and on-hardware testing that keeps production-ready software shipping reliably.

This role is designed to grow with you, and we're hiring across a range of experience levels. Earlier-career engineers will start by expanding test coverage, reducing flaky tests, and supporting structured QA and dogfooding while learning our stack and robotics hardware. With more experience, you will own the end-to-end QA process — test infrastructure, performance tooling, CI/CD test stages, and on-hardware deployment and testing — and drive quality strategy across the engineering team.

You'll also work as a software engineer on the team, contributing bug fixes and features alongside your testing work. You’ll partner closely with other engineers to improve testability and turn manual findings into automated regression tests. This role combines solid software engineering skills, a tester's adversarial curiosity, and a commitment to shipping reliable software.



Am I a good fit?

You:

 - Understand the value and strength of automation in testing

 - Adversarially approach coding

 - Enjoy creative thinking when approaching software development

 - Have a passion for debugging

 - Care about metrics, efficiency, and process improvement

 - Love robotics and care deeply about customer success with our product

 - Want to uplevel the team around you



Requirements

 - Bachelor's in Robotics, Computer Science, or a related field

 - Hands-on experience testing a physical or robotics product or platform: application and system-level testing against real hardware behavior, including on-hardware testing and deployment

 - Experience with Docker and understanding of CI/CD concepts

 - Familiarity with at least one testing framework

 - An adversarial, quality-first mindset: the judgment to decide what to test and to own whether the product is actually correct, through exploratory and edge-case testing rather than just keeping the build green

 - Foundational software and robotics skills

 - Basic C++ experience and familiarity with Python

 - Top-notch communication skills and time management

 - Ability to think critically, ask good questions, and move confidently when troubleshooting

 - Able to travel approximately every other month to customer sites & conferences (<20%)

 - Live in the Boulder/Denver area with the ability to be in-office, especially for hands-on, on-hardware testing



Some preferred skills/experience:

source_jobs.title: Robotics Software Engineer II (Boulder, CO)
source_jobs.description:
PickNik Robotics is redefining the robotics industry with MoveIt Pro, a first-of-its-kind platform that combines AI and motion planning to make robots work in the real world. Physical AI is reshaping the field: as foundation models and vision-language-action (VLA) policies move from the lab into production, MoveIt Pro combines these learned approaches with robust, safety-aware planning and control so robots can reliably perform real tasks. Built on over a decade of experience with companies like GM, Philips, Samsung, BMW, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and leading startups, MoveIt Pro brings proven capabilities into production. Join us in helping companies deploy AI to robots quickly and effectively, unlocking new possibilities. This is a challenging, high-impact role with the opportunity to do some of the most rewarding work of your career.

PickNik is an agentic-coding-forward company. We use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as part of our everyday development to ship and maintain high-quality software faster, and we expect engineers to adopt these tools and keep getting more effective with them.

As a Robotics Software Engineer at PickNik Robotics, you will help scope, design, and develop scalable improvements to the MoveIt Pro platform — writing software that runs on robots around the world (and in outer space!). The work spans the full stack, from robot hardware integration and motion planning through to the platform features and APIs that customers build on. Increasingly, that means bringing physical AI into production by integrating learned policies and foundation models, including vision-language-action (VLA) models, alongside the classical planning and control that make robots dependable. It requires both a strategic mindset to design singular solutions that fulfill multiple use cases and the forethought to anticipate and test the edge cases that matter before they reach production. Our development is driven by users in dozens of industries with robots spread across prototype, pilot, and production environments.



Am I a good fit?

 - You love robotics and have a passion for creating the next generation of robot software.

 - Are excited to be part of a team of robotics experts.

 - Should work efficiently, be ready to learn, and have great attention to detail.

 - Desire to contribute to closed and open source projects solving big problems.

 - Ability to collaborate with teams around the world.



Requirements

 - Driven to ship reliable software used in production settings to solve hard problems robotics have never done before.

 - Flexibility across a number of tasks beyond developing code, such as debugging performance, pair programming on multidisciplinary regressions, testing CUDA compatibility, and investigating user pain points.

 - Minimum 4 years of professional work experience.

 - Strong C++17 (or newer) experience and familiarity with at least one scripting language, preferably Python.

 - Experience with JavaScript, especially in interaction with ROS 2 applications.

 - Experience with robot arm hardware integration, such as trajectory controller development and driver development for commercial hardware.

 - Extensive knowledge of software development in Linux.

 - Experience with ROS 2.

 - Experience with full life-cycle library and application development, Git version control, software testing, and continuous integration.

 - Be able to travel approximately every other month to customer sites & conferences. Less than 20% of the time.

 - Live in the Boulder/Denver area and have the ability to be in-office at least 3 days per week.

 - Degree(s) in Computer Science, Robotics, Engineering, or a related field and/or equivalent industry experience. Advanced degrees are a plus.



Some preferred skills/experience

 - Experience with multiple robot description formats, such as URDF and MJCF.

 - Experience with ros2_control hardware interfaces for 500+ Hz drivers.

 - Experience shipping and maintaining a so

source_jobs.title: Robotics Support Lead (Boulder, CO)
source_jobs.description:
PickNik Robotics is redefining the robotics industry with MoveIt Pro, a first-of-its-kind platform that combines AI and motion planning to make robots work in the real world. Physical AI is reshaping the field: as foundation models and vision-language-action (VLA) policies move from the lab into production, MoveIt Pro combines these learned approaches with robust, safety-aware planning and control so robots can reliably perform real tasks. Built on over a decade of experience with companies like GM, Philips, Samsung, BMW, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and leading startups, MoveIt Pro brings proven capabilities into production. Join us in helping companies deploy AI to robots quickly and effectively, unlocking new possibilities. This is a challenging, high-impact role with the opportunity to do some of the most rewarding work of your career.

As Support Lead at PickNik Robotics, you will own and elevate the MoveIt Pro support function. You will serve as both a hands-on support engineer and the leader of the support rotation, ensuring that customers receive fast, high-quality responses while driving continuous improvements to our product and processes.

You will coordinate complex customer situations involving multiple concurrent issues, prioritize based on business impact, and ensure clear communication internally and externally. You will partner closely with engineering and product to characterize bugs, influence roadmap priorities, and improve usability and documentation.

This role combines deep technical skill, customer empathy, and operational leadership.



Am I a good fit?

You:

 - Take ownership when something is unclear or stuck

 - Are calm in high-pressure situations, bring structure to chaos

 - Communicate clearly and confidently with both engineers and executives

 - Enjoy challenges: leadership challenges, problem solving challenges, organizational challenges

 - Love robotics and care deeply about customer success

 - Care about metrics, efficiency, and process improvement

 - Want to uplevel the team around you



Requirements

 - Troubleshooting ability: think critically, assume nothing, get data, and always work the problem

 - Top notch communication skills and time management

 - Willingness to work with AI tools and learn on your feet

 - Bachelor’s in Robotics, Computer Science, or related field

 - Ability to automate and improve processes

 - Some C++ experience, familiarity with Python and Docker



Some preferred skills/experience

 - Demonstrated leadership or team coordination experience

 - Basic experience with Machine Learning

 - MoveIt, ROS, Nav2, Mujoco, PCL, OpenCV, or other robotic libraries are advantageous

 - Experience leading support or customer engineering teams

 - Experience working with customers

 - Experience improving documentation and internal processes

 - Machine Learning frameworks such as ONNX and PyTorch



Benefits:

 - Health, Dental, and Vision insurance

 - 401k Matching

 - Paid time off

 - Opportunity to share in the company’s success through equity



Minimum Salary

 - The minimum salary for this position is $120,000/year.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer (Boulder, CO)
source_jobs.description:
PickNik Robotics is redefining the robotics industry with MoveIt Pro, a first-of-its-kind platform that combines AI and motion planning to make robots work in the real world. Physical AI is reshaping the field: as foundation models and vision-language-action (VLA) policies move from the lab into production, MoveIt Pro combines these learned approaches with robust, safety-aware planning and control so robots can reliably perform real tasks. Built on over a decade of experience with companies like GM, Philips, Samsung, BMW, Lockheed Martin, NASA, and leading startups, MoveIt Pro brings proven capabilities into production. Join us in helping companies deploy AI to robots quickly and effectively, unlocking new possibilities. This is a challenging, high-impact role with the opportunity to do some of the most rewarding work of your career.

PickNik is an agentic-coding-forward company. We use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as part of our everyday development to ship and maintain high-quality software faster, and we expect engineers to adopt these tools and keep getting more effective with them.

As a Staff Software Engineer at PickNik Robotics, you will be the software architect for the MoveIt Pro integration backbone — the layer that turns the work of our robotics specialty experts into a platform that powers robots around the world and in space. You will own the architecture of our behavior tree framework, bridge layer, supporting backend services, and the interfaces with the frontend. You will coordinate between robotics specialty experts and frontend engineers to ship cross-cutting features. As physical AI reshapes how robots are programmed — with foundation models and vision-language-action (VLA) policies joining classical planning and control — you will ensure the integration backbone enables these approaches to work together reliably in production.

Expert software design, system architecture, and API design are essential for this role, along with deep C++ expertise. You will also be a champion of agentic coding at PickNik, both as a strong daily user of these tools, and a mentor to the team on using them effectively and safely. You will collaborate closely with robotics specialty experts to build a robotics software platform, so any robotics domain experience is a welcome plus.

Am I a good fit?

 - You love robotics and want to define the architecture of a platform powering robots around the world.

 - You have owned large codebases end to end and have strong opinions on system design, API design, and long-term maintainability.

 - You enjoy the integrator role - defining boundaries between subsystems and making specialized work fit together cleanly.

 - You are a power user of agentic coding tools and enjoy helping a team adopt them.

 - You have the ability to collaborate with teams around the world.

Requirements

 - Degree(s) in Computer Science, Engineering, Robotics, or a related field, and/or equivalent industry experience. Advanced degrees are a plus.

 - Minimum 8 years of professional software engineering experience

 - Expert-level C++ (C++20 or newer), including modern patterns, concurrency, and performance work.

 - Demonstrated architectural ownership of a non-trivial system — design documents, design reviews, API and contract design, and defining the boundaries and contracts between subsystems for long-term maintainability.

 - Strong cross-disciplinary debugging skills — able to trace problems across language, subsystem, and stack boundaries to find a root cause rather than a symptom.

 - Strong Python for backend services, tooling, and infrastructure.

 - Linux-native full-lifecycle library and application development experience, including CMake, Docker, Git, software testing, and continuous integration.

 - Hands-on experience with agentic coding tools (such as Claude Code, Cursor, or equivalent) and a track record of using them effectively on production code.

 - Be able to travel to client sites &

notes: Robotics-focused company with a strong engineering core
